title: evangelion chronicles kinda reviewed
wikitext:
[]: evangelion chronicles- "kinda" reviewed             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 07, 2006 8:36 am hurray! the evamonkey forum is back online! as one of the few readers of this site who actually lives in tokyo, i was able to get my hands on the first of the series "evangelion chronicles", a magazine priced at 590yen (tax incl. and cheaper than the reported 690yen for the first issue only) which comes to about $5.50 for 32 colored pages. the pages are all hole-punched so you could stick it in your "evangelion chronicles" binder sold separately for approx. 1,550yen ($15); which is recommended since there'll be 30 issues and unless you're a hell of a housekeeper, you'll lose one or two and end up crying in a corner of your room. as for the content, i would say it's surprisingly good for a blatant way of milking the eva cash cow (oh come on 30 issues and the binder will cost you a good $210.. not to mention shipping fees). there are many diagrams, new artwork, retouched artwork, and detailed explanations of the eva-world from a full strategy and tactic analysis of the sachiel battle (aka. 3rd angel) to the way tokyo-3 is operated and it's full history. i've not read every single official article and canon given by gainax, but i'm pretty sure this is new stuff and as the last few issues are released, maybe reichu's theories will be proven (big fan of her work). to the meat of this post: the pics. i dont have a scanner so i just took pics of some pages. keep in mind that i'm a bad photographer. the cover page as advertised contents Shinji yes, they even have 2 full pages dedicated to the female operators of nerv. apparently gendo decided that apart from aoba and shigeru, all operators must be female and not ugly. they say he's a smart man. details on the 3rd angel battle new artwork regarding tokyo-3's function pretty cool eh? and of course, a page dedicated to eva products if i feel like it, i'll translate some new information within the coming days. [/long post]

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 07, 2006 9:19 am Ooooh. Pretty. :o I want... I want... If you see anything interesting, please bring it to our attention. Are you intending collect the entire run of this?

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 07, 2006 9:37 am im trying to do so but unfortunately i'm leaving tokyo in august to attend university in new york, which means i could only get the first 3, but im trying to get in contact with gainax to see if i could arrange an overseas subscription via snail mail. if i succeed i'll make sure ill let the readers know (since you could probably do the same) but im willing to bet that a third party website would be more convenient (e.g. amazon.co.jp- books are not internationally restricted). anyways im halfway through translating an article from the chronicle about the people who left the katsuragi expedition on sep. 12 2000. i got a full free day tomorrow so ill be posting quite a few translations. don't expect fancy/perfect translations like bochan bird's or reichu's... i'm still learning Japanese. :? you think i should include the original Japanese text with my translation just in case?

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sat Jul 08, 2006 12:52 am Cool.... I'm waiting for the translation....

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Sat Jul 08, 2006 7:48 am Finally here it is... my first translation. Don't expect any MIND BLOWING revelations yet because like most good women, Gainax doesn't give it all out on the first date/volume. You do get tidbits of new and redundant inofrmation though. ***disclaimer: This is not a 100% direct, word-for-word translation of the original text. This translation was done without referring to the ADV translation of episode 21â€™. Taken from the â€œTimeline Sheetâ€ section of Evangelion Chronicle; volume 1, Page 01-14. A.D. 2000, September 12th- Antarctica Part of the Katsuragi Expedition returns home. In Antarctica, the S2 Engineâ€™s development proceeded smoothly owing to Dr.　Katsuragi. Though it is only a hypothesis, if the S2 Engine is realized it will bring immeasurable benefits. To that end they must make the research fruitful as soon as possible or else... Within the research team a feverish atmosphere made its presence and progress for the research was shown, but suddenly a group of people who joined the expedition returned to the motherland. They left Antarctica without letting anyone around them know, and didnâ€™t plan on telling anyone why. Within this group, were Keel and Gendo. Although the two of them joined the expedition, they joined according to Seeleâ€™s plans. â€œScientists, they believe in themselves too much.â€ â€œTheyâ€™re self-righteous?â€ â€œTheyâ€™re too subjective; a group of people who canâ€™t accurately grasp reality.â€ â€œAnd they want (the) truth. What ironic peopleâ€¦â€ The conversation between Keel and Gendo seems to forecast the tragedy that happened afterwards. (Caption for the sketches of young Gendo and Keel.) When Gendo and Keel were both young they joined the Katsuragi Expedition, but their goal was not the completion of the S2 Engine. Instead they established connections to a humanoid called â€œAdamâ€, who was sleeping under Antarctica. ***Original Japanese text*** A.D.2000　９月１２日・南極 葛城調査隊の一部の人間が帰国 南極に渡った葛城博士によってS2機関の開発は順調に進められていた。仮説の階段とはいえ、S2機関が実現したとすれば、それがもたらす恩恵は計り知れないものになる。そのために一日も早く研究成果をものにしなければ・・・・・・。調査隊の間には熱病にも似た空気が満ち、研究は進展の度合いを見せていた。ところが突然、調査隊に参加した一部の人間が母国へ帰還してしまったのだ。彼らは周囲に知られることなく南極を後にし、誰にも帰国理由を話そうとはしなかった。そんな帰国者のなかにキールとゲンドウの姿があった。ふたりも調査隊に参加していたのだが、彼らはゼーレの計画のために行動していたのだ。「科学者ってぇのは、どうも自分の考えを信じ過ぎるからね」「独善的ですか」「思い込みが激し過ぎるんだよ。現実を的確に把握できない連中だからね」「そういう連中が真実を求めている。皮肉なものです・・・・・・」キールとゲンドウの会話は、この後に起こる惨劇を予測しているかのようだった。 (Caption for the sketches of young Gendo and Keel.) まだ若いゲンドウとキールの両名も葛城調査隊に参加していた。だが彼らの目的はS2機関の完成ではなく、南極の地下に眠る「アダム」と呼ばれるヒト型成物に関係していた。 ***Original Source*** More translations when I find something interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Sat Jul 08, 2006 9:55 am The way Evangelion Chronicle is laid out is in a sense, pretty random. There are two or three pages and maybe a centerfold for each section (mechanic, character, timeline, tactics, installation, technology, extra) so the information given is quite broad, which is how I jumped from "the Katsuragi Expedition" to "Development of Tokyo-3" The Development of Tokyo 3 Second Impact, the unprecedented disaster that occurred on the 13th of September, 2000, is said to have been caused by huge meteors that landed at tens of percents of the speed of light. The result of the disaster was that Earthâ€™s axis became tilted, leading to abnormal weather throughout the world. Furthermore, in politically instable regions, disputes and tribal confrontations became apparent and all over the world disturbances of war arose. Japan was not able to escape from these effects and on September 20th of the same year, terrorists dropped an n2 bomb onto Old Tokyo (Tokyo-1/Former Tokyo). Due to the destruction of Old Tokyo, Matsumoto City of the Nagano Prefecture (Tokyo-2) was selected as the replacement capital of Japan. Incidentally, it was announced to the public that Tokyo-2 was only a temporary capital, and that there were plans underway to move all of the functions of the capital to Tokyo-3 in due time. Simple Timeline/ Tokyo-3â€™s Developmental History A.D.2000.9.13 .The Second Impact occurred .Antarctica disappeared causing the sea level to rise by more than ten meters. .Most of Old Tokyo is lost to the ocean. 9.20 .Terrorists dropped an n2 bomb onto Old Tokyo (Tokyo-1/Former Tokyo) .Functions of the capital is lost A.D.2001 .Plans to relocate the capital began .Matsumoto City of the Nagano Prefecture was chosen as the new capital .Construction of Tokyo-2 began 2.14 .â€œSt. Valentineâ€™s Temporary Peace Treatyâ€ was signed .The â€œ(Japanese) Self Defence Forceâ€ was commissioned by the United Nations .â€œTactical Self Defence Forceâ€ was inaugurated A.D.2003 .Tokyo-2 was completed A.D.2005 .The Chairman of the United Nations started plans for Tokyo-3 A.D.2010 .The 7th Generation Super Computer Magi was activated .Gehirn was dissolved .United Nationâ€™s Secret Organization NERV was inaugurated .NERV sets its base in under Tokyo-3 (Geofront) ***Original Japanese text*** 第３新東京市の成立 西暦２０００年９月１３日に発生した末曾有の大災害「セカンドインパクト」は、光速の数十％にも及ぶ速度で飛来した大質量隕石が南極大陸に衝突したために引き起こされたものと言われる。その結果、地球の地軸は傾き、世界的な異常気像が発生。さらに政情不安地域を中心に紛争や民族間の対立が表面化し、世界各地で戦乱が巻き起こってしまった。日本もその影響を免れることができず、同年９月２０日、テロリストが旧東京にｎ２爆弾を投下。これにより旧東京は壊滅し、それに代わる新たな首都として長野県松本市（第２新東京市）が選出されたのだった。ちなみに表向きには第２新東京市は臨時首都であり、ゆくゆくは第３新東京市に首都機能を移管する計画は進められている。 簡易年表/第三新東京市　成立史 A.D.2000.9.13 .セカンドインパクト　発生 .南極消失により海面が十数ｍ程度上昇 .旧東京の大半が海中に沈む。 9.20 .テロリストが旧東京にｎ２爆弾を投下 .首都機能が失われる A.D.2001 .遷都計画が発動 .長野県松本市が新たな首都に選定される .第２新東京市　建設開始 2.14 .バレンタイン休戦臨時条約　結締 .自衛隊が国連軍に委託される .戦略自衛隊　発足 　　 A.D.2003 .第２新東京市　完成 A.D.2005 .国連主導による第３新東京市開発計画が発動 A.D.2010 .第７代スーパーコンピュータ、MAGI初起動 .ゲヒルン解体 .国連非公開特務組織NERV発足 .NERV, 第３新東京市地下（ジオフロント）に居を構える ***Original Source*** [rant] So I just realized that with 30 issues planned, Gainax NEEDS to come up with new information... even if it means pulling it out their backsides. Take for example, the "character" section of Evangelion Chronicle- this issue we meet Shinji and the female operators of Nerv. Two characters per issue? That's not gona last till the last issue. Not even if it were 1 character per issue. There aren't 30 characters worth writing about in Evangelion, and obviously not 60. So what will happen? No clue. But I sure as hell don't want to read about NERV's Kitchen Staff and Hygiene Technicians (Read: Janitors). That's not the only section- how about "Mechanics"? There are only 13 Evas and JA.... and I'm sure weapons will be covered in the "Technology" section. [/rant] Next up- what Evangelion Chronicle has to say about Evangelions. (This is a loooong article so it might take anywhere from a day to half a week to get this done.) Until then, please give me some feedback on my translation if you know any Japanese. Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 08, 2006 10:39 am It doesn't really make anything new (Except for Keel's and Gendo's little chat, I guess), but it's nice to see everything neat and organized, I suppose. Good job!

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Sat Jul 08, 2006 10:46 am Haha thanks Legend, but not even Keel and Gendo's little chat is new. It was from the start of EP 21' (director's cut) hidden beneath a slew of background chatter. If you want a full transcript of it go to [url]www.evacommentary.org[/url] I'm a little disappointed at Eva Chronicles too, but in retrospect it never promised to be full of new information (though it did promise to explain everything) and being great capitalizers, Gainax is sure to leave the best for the last. Never show everything at first.. eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryousanki King » Sat Jul 08, 2006 11:57 am Hehe. I wish I lived in Japan just so I could grab this. The production art is delicious for someone like me who has yet to buy any EVA books other than the manga. :P

Never show everything at first.. eh?

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Sat Jul 08, 2006 2:31 pm どうもありがとうございます for the feedback Reichu様！ I'm really having trouble trying to keep the translation coherent by ironing out the Engrish, and keeping the juicy content as it was. You know what I mean Just guessing the pronounciations of all the kanji when I typed up the Japanese text was hell... thank god i speak cantonese and they sound similar. reichu wrote: Do you think there's any way for poor, unfortunate foreigners to subscribe to this thing, or do we have to pick up the issues individually?

But than again why let a non-human be in charge of the government....of course that begs the question why is a non-human in charge of the Human Completion Project...

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE 08 » Sun Jul 09, 2006 12:40 am Actually the Red Cross Book says he's mechanical from the spine down, I'd say he's pretty much a machine at this point. I'd call that a cyborg not a human.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Sun Jul 09, 2006 1:32 am Okay so let's define cyborg before we decide whether poor Keel is cyborg or human. Dictionary.com wrote: cyÂ·borg n. A human who has certain physiological processes aided or controlled by mechanical or electronic devices.

[]:             Quote Post by BLACKANGEL32076 » Tue Jul 11, 2006 10:51 am Any chance of this coming to the U.S. in English?

chunks.content:
Article: evangelion chronicles kinda reviewed
[]: evangelion chronicles- "kinda" reviewed             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 07, 2006 8:36 am hurray! the evamonkey forum is back online! as one of the few readers of this site who actually lives in tokyo, i was able to get my hands on the first of the series "evangelion chronicles", a magazine priced at 590yen (tax incl. and cheaper than the reported 690yen for the first issue only) which comes to about $5.50 for 32 colored pages. the pages are all hole-punched so you could stick it in your "evangelion chronicles" binder sold separately for approx. 1,550yen ($15); which is recommended since there'll be 30 issues and unless you're a hell of a housekeeper, you'll lose one or two and end up crying in a corner of your room. as for the content, i would say it's surprisingly good for a blatant way of milking the eva cash cow (oh come on 30 issues and the binder will cost you a good $210.. not to mention shipping fees). there are many diagrams, new artwork, retouched artwork, and detailed explanations of the eva-world from a full strategy and tactic analysis of the sachiel battle (aka. 3rd angel) to the way tokyo-3 is operated and it's full history. i've not read every single official article and canon given by gainax, but i'm pretty sure this is new stuff and as the last few issues are released, maybe reichu's theories will be proven (big fan of her work). to the meat of this post: the pics. i dont have a scanner so i just took pics of some pages. keep in mind that i'm a bad photographer. the cover page as advertised contents Shinji yes, they even have 2 full pages dedicated to the female operators of nerv. apparently gendo decided that apart from aoba and shigeru, all operators must be female and not ugly. they say he's a smart man. details on the 3rd angel battle new artwork regarding tokyo-3's function pretty cool eh? and of course, a page dedicated to eva products if i feel like it, i'll translate some new information within the coming days. [/long post]

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 07,

Article: evangelion chronicles kinda reviewed
 of course, a page dedicated to eva products if i feel like it, i'll translate some new information within the coming days. [/long post]

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jul 07, 2006 9:19 am Ooooh. Pretty. :o I want... I want... If you see anything interesting, please bring it to our attention. Are you intending collect the entire run of this?

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 07, 2006 9:37 am im trying to do so but unfortunately i'm leaving tokyo in august to attend university in new york, which means i could only get the first 3, but im trying to get in contact with gainax to see if i could arrange an overseas subscription via snail mail. if i succeed i'll make sure ill let the readers know (since you could probably do the same) but im willing to bet that a third party website would be more convenient (e.g. amazon.co.jp- books are not internationally restricted). anyways im halfway through translating an article from the chronicle about the people who left the katsuragi expedition on sep. 12 2000. i got a full free day tomorrow so ill be posting quite a few translations. don't expect fancy/perfect translations like bochan bird's or reichu's... i'm still learning Japanese. :? you think i should include the original Japanese text with my translation just in case?

To that end they must make the research fruitful as soon as possible or else... Within the research team a feverish atmosphere made its presence and progress for the research was shown, but suddenly a group of people who joined the expedition returned to the motherland. They left Antarctica without letting anyone around them know, and didnâ€™t plan on telling anyone why. Within this group, were Keel and Gendo. Article: evangelion chronicles kinda reviewed
滅し、それに代わる新たな首都として長野県松本市（第２新東京市）が選出されたのだった。ちなみに表向きには第２新東京市は臨時首都であり、ゆくゆくは第３新東京市に首都機能を移管する計画は進められている。 簡易年表/第三新東京市　成立史 A.D.2000.9.13 .セカンドインパクト　発生 .南極消失により海面が十数ｍ程度上昇 .旧東京の大半が海中に沈む。 9.20 .テロリストが旧東京にｎ２爆弾を投下 .首都機能が失われる A.D.2001 .遷都計画が発動 .長野県松本市が新たな首都に選定される .第２新東京市　建設開始 2.14 .バレンタイン休戦臨時条約　結締 .自衛隊が国連軍に委託される .戦略自衛隊　発足 　　 A.D.2003 .第２新東京市　完成 A.D.2005 .国連主導による第３新東京市開発計画が発動 A.D.2010 .第７代スーパーコンピュータ、MAGI初起動 .ゲヒルン解体 .国連非公開特務組織NERV発足 .NERV, 第３新東京市地下（ジオフロント）に居を構える ***Original Source*** [rant] So I just realized that with 30 issues planned, Gainax NEEDS to come up with new information... even if it means pulling it out their backsides. Article: evangelion chronicles kinda reviewed
��える ***Original Source*** [rant] So I just realized that with 30 issues planned, Gainax NEEDS to come up with new information... even if it means pulling it out their backsides. Take for example, the "character" section of Evangelion Chronicle- this issue we meet Shinji and the female operators of Nerv. Two characters per issue? That's not gona last till the last issue. Not even if it were 1 character per issue. There aren't 30 characters worth writing about in Evangelion, and obviously not 60. So what will happen? No clue. But I sure as hell don't want to read about NERV's Kitchen Staff and Hygiene Technicians (Read: Janitors). That's not the only section- how about "Mechanics"? There are only 13 Evas and JA.... and I'm sure weapons will be covered in the "Technology" section. [/rant] Next up- what Evangelion Chronicle has to say about Evangelions. (This is a loooong article so it might take anywhere from a day to half a week to get this done.) Until then, please give me some feedback on my translation if you know any Japanese. Thanks!
